dial.summary: Net macro score holds at +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 ~$102.7T on intact 4-week uptrend through May 2026 data, Fed balance sheet $6.74T confirmed flat per H.4.1 week ended July 15, BTC funding +0.01% near-zero, stablecoin dominance 13.32% in confirmed 30-day downtrend from ~14.4% in mid-June; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above 50 but falling ahead of August 3 July release, 2s10s +0.41% positive but flat 4-week; 2 headwinds: DXY 100.78 rebounded intraday July 17 after the post-CPI/PPI selloff and remains above the 100 headwind threshold, real yields 2.33% elevated and still rising on a 4-week basis); BTC.D 56.3% firm, TOTAL ~$2.28T stable; the July 28–29 FOMC is the next macro catalyst with a hold expected at 3.5–3.75% and focus on Chair Warsh's language on the disinflation pipeline, which if dovish could compress real yields toward neutral and flip DXY below 100—both remaining headwinds.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: MacroMicro 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at ~$102.7T through May 2026—the latest available data point given the 6–8 week reporting lag; June 2026 aggregate data not yet published; 4-week trend up within the May dataset: rose ~$1.0T from ~$101.7T (~30 days prior in the series) to ~$102.7T, a +~1.0% gain well above the 0.5% flat threshold; US M2 alone at $23.05T (May 2026, next release late July); status supportive (4-week trend clearly up within available data, YoY growth positive per the Mises Institute which noted multi-year high money-supply growth in 2026 as Fed balance sheet held flat); value unchanged this run—data lag, no June 2026 aggregate published yet.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI confirmed at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion but 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; no July 2026 data—next ISM Manufacturing PMI releases August 3, 2026; 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (still contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022, consistent with the June CPI and June PPI disinflationary signal); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires both above 50 AND rising; headwind threshold requires below 48 and falling); value unchanged this run—no new ISM data until August 3.

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY at 100.78 on July 17, 2026 (+0.06% from previous session), rebounding from the 100.40 area after the back-to-back June CPI (July 14) and June PPI (July 15) soft prints that drove the initial selloff; 4-week trend flat—DXY was near ~100.30 on June 17 (post-FOMC), now at 100.78, a change of +0.48 points; relative change of +0.48% is just under the 0.5% flat threshold so trend is classified flat; status headwind (ICE DXY at 100.78 is above the 100 headwind threshold; a sustained close below 100 is required to flip to neutral—the July 28–29 FOMC and Chair Warsh's tone on disinflation progress is the next catalyst that could push DXY back toward or below 100).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.33% as of July 16, 2026 (most recent FRED daily observation, up +0.03pp from July 15); 4-week trend up—DFII10 has risen ~0.15pp from approximately 2.18% on June 17 (post-FOMC) to 2.33%, well above the 5bp flat threshold; June CPI (3.5% YoY) and soft June PPI prints have begun to compress nominal 10Y yields toward 4.57%, but breakevens have moved in tandem, so the real yield compression has been modest; a sustained move to 2.00% or below is required to flip from headwind to neutral; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis); value unchanged from prior run—awaiting July 17 DFII10 observation.

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.57% and 2-year at 4.16% as of July 16, 2026 (latest confirmed data)—spread +0.41%, narrowed 2bps from the prior session's +0.43%; 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39% on June 17 (post-FOMC), now +0.41%, a +2bp change well within the 5bps flat threshold; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the -50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold, but not yet sustaining a steepening trend across the full 30-day window—supportive requires positive AND steepening; the July 28–29 FOMC hold with dovish Warsh language could steepen the curve toward a neutral-to-supportive flip).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 balance sheet for week ended July 15, 2026 (released July 17): total assets $6,743,028M ($6.743T, rounded to $6.74T); up $7.4B from the prior week (July 9: $6,735,609M) and ~$73–80B above year-ago levels; 4-week comparison: week ended ~June 17 estimated ~$6.71T; 4-week change ~+$33B / +0.49%, just within the flat threshold; status supportive (balance sheet flat with no acceleration of QT, no aggressive reserve drain, year-over-year growth modestly positive).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC perpetual funding rate held near +0.01% per 8-hour interval through mid-July 2026 (Binance/Bybit/OKX aggregate via CoinGlass); specific July 17 real-time rate could not be sourced this run—value unchanged from prior run; with BTC.D at 56.3% and TOTAL at ~$2.28T stable, the absence of elevated funding rates confirms the spot-driven bid rather than leveraged long accumulation; status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during stable TOTAL market cap is consistent with healthy BTC accumulation dynamics).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$305B against total crypto market cap ~$2.28T as of July 17, 2026 (CoinGecko global charts: 13.32%); up slightly from 13.22% on July 16 but the 30-day downtrend remains clearly intact—dominance was ~14.4% in mid-June and has fallen ~1.1pp over 30 days; a single-session uptick of 0.10pp does not constitute a trend reversal given the full 30-day context; BTC.D at 56.3% and stablecoin dominance declining 30-day is consistent with capital rotation into BTC rather than broad altcoin season; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition requires rising stablecoin dominance while TOTAL crypto market cap is also rising, which does not apply here—TOTAL stable at $2.28T while dominance trends lower).

upcoming.title: NAHB Housing Market Index (July 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Home Builders / Wells Fargo Housing Market Index for July 2026 at 10:00 AM ET—builder sentiment leading indicator; FOMC blackout runs July 18–30 so no Fed commentary accompanies this week's releases; first trading day of a quiet macro week that builds toward the ECB decision (July 23), Flash PMIs (July 24), and ultimately the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: Existing Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Realtors (NAR) Existing Home Sales for June 2026—closings on previously owned homes reflecting mortgage lock-in dynamics in a still-elevated rate environment (10Y at 4.57%, TIPS real yield 2.33%); released within the FOMC blackout (July 18–30) so no Fed amplification; one trading day before the ECB rate decision on July 23, which will set cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: ECB Rate Decision (July 2026)
upcoming.note: ECB Governing Council decision at 13:45 CET (7:45 AM ET), press conference at 14:30 CET (8:30 AM ET)—July is a non-projection meeting (no updated Staff Economic Projections); ECB deposit facility rate at 2.25% (raised 25bps at the June 11 meeting); markets price a ~93% probability of a hold at July 23; Lagarde's press conference tone is the primary market driver given the non-projection format; will assess whether global disinflation (US CPI 3.5% YoY, soft US PPI) and post-ceasefire commodity easing reduce the urgency for further action; ECB guidance sets cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 19, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET—same morning as ECB decision; last jobless claims print before the July 28–29 FOMC rate decision (announced Wednesday July 29 at 2:00 PM ET); two consecutive weeks above 240K would materially shift rate-cut pricing entering the Wednesday announcement; the ECB + claims combination on July 23 is the most consequential macro morning before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.

upcoming.title: S&P Global Flash US PMI / New Home Sales (July 2026)
upcoming.note: S&P Global Preliminary (Flash) US Manufacturing and Services PMI for July 2026 at ~9:45 AM ET—first comprehensive business activity read for Q3, released four days before the July 28–29 FOMC; a manufacturing flash PMI above 53 consistent with the June ISM (53.3%) would signal expansion broadening into Q3; services PMI is watched for sticky wage-driven services inflation; Census Bureau New Home Sales (June 2026) also at 10:00 AM ET; these are among the last major indicators before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.
